required_evidence: Measured nut-to-qianjin and qianjin-to-bridge distances on a reference erhu plus photo capture filed alongside resonator-photo-measurement-evidence.csv
notes: Setup-strip DXF controls qianjin-to-bridge only and does not authorize total neck length or nut/scroll reference position

required_evidence: Measured cross-section at qianjin nut and mid-neck plus playing-surface profile photos
notes: Cross-section must come from measurement or shop-reviewed CAD not concept visuals

required_evidence: Measured peg taper angle shaft diameter bore diameter and seat depth for both inner and outer string pegs
notes: Friction-peg seat tolerance controls tuning stability and must be measured before any bore or reamer spec is promoted

required_evidence: Measured or reviewed scroll/head profile with peg-axis spacing and overall head length
notes: Head shape may be stylistic but peg-axis spacing controls string geometry and must be measured

required_evidence: Measured spike (qin tong) length below resonator plus seat geometry into the resonator wall
notes: Spike length affects player ergonomics and resonator coupling and must not be inferred from neck length alone

required_evidence: Measured bridge height foot footprint mass and string-notch spacing filed alongside membrane-bridge-contact-capture.csv
notes: Companion to RES-GATE-004 which governs contact area on the membrane; this gate governs the bridge object itself

required_evidence: Measured string spacing at qianjin and at bridge for inner and outer course
notes: String spacing controls bow clearance and intonation and must be measured not copied from setup-strip DXF

required_evidence: Shop-reviewed DXF/CAD derived from measured neck peg scroll spike bridge and string-spacing data
notes: Current DXF authorizes only setup-strip qianjin-to-bridge marks; neck peg scroll spike and bridge geometry remain unauthorized for fabrication until this gate clears
